steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the structure of the passage: it opens with a specific historical example (Clara Benton's 1867 journalism), then describes an organization that preserves and honors that history (the NABJ).

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — It summarizes a theory about journalism, then explains how a journalistic practice has changed over time.: the passage does not summarize a theory or discuss how a journalistic practice changed over time; it moves from Benton's specific achievement to the NABJ's preservation of Black journalistic history. ✗
  • Choice B — It mentions a specific achievement in Black journalistic history, then describes an organization dedicated to upholding that history.: matches exactly — Benton's 1867 work is the specific achievement; the NABJ, founded to honor journalists like Benton, is the organization. ✓
  • Choice C — It introduces the history of an organization honoring Black journalists, then suggests how that organization might recruit more members.: the passage does not suggest recruiting members; it says the NABJ was founded to support Black media professionals and honor journalists like Benton. ✗
  • Choice D — It describes the career of a well-known figure in Black journalism, then compares that career to one of a figure who is lesser known.: the passage does not compare Benton's career with another journalist's career. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
